seo_title: Aerial Inspection Behaviors via RL-based Quadrotor Control for…
meta_description: This paper presents an end-to-end Quadrotor control policy that achieves inspection view-pose tracking in under-canopy forest environments, utilizing a higher…
body_html:
The paper presents an end-to-end Quadrotor control policy that achieves inspection view-pose tracking in under-canopy forest environments, utilizing a higher navigation guidance layer.

Key Components of the Proposed System

  • A Traveling Salesman Problem planner (TSP) to find the optimal visitation sequence
  • A Rapidly-exploring Random Tree Star (RRT*) planner to generate collision-free paths
  • An RL-based motor-level stabilizing controller for low-level inspection execution

Benefits of the Proposed System

Feature Description
Inspection View-Pose Tracking Simultaneous position and yaw reference tracking for various target inspection behaviors and point-to-point navigation in forests
Collision-Free Paths Generated by an RRT* planner to respect the tracking limitations of the lower end-to-end RL policy
Safety and Reliability Ensured through the utilization of a higher navigation guidance layer comprising of TSP and RRT* planners

Conclusion

The paper demonstrates that an RL-based motor-level stabilizing controller, supported by a navigation guidance layer, can be used effectively as the low-level inspection execution module for under-canopy forest inspection missions.

source.title: Aerial Inspection Behaviors via RL-based Quadrotor Control for Under-canopy Forest Environments
source.summary: Researchers have developed a deep Reinforcement Learning (RL)-based control system for quadrotors to navigate and inspect under-canopy forests. The system uses an end-to-end policy that tracks the view pose of the quadrotor, allowing it to track targets and navigate through the forest safely. A navigation guidance layer is also used to plan collision-free paths between inspection regions. The system has been successfully tested in five target inspection scenarios.
